ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food product code identified for raw pig lung; SNOMED CT coverage of pork organ meats is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of pig lung is approximately 100 g, often prepared by slicing and blanching before use in soups or braised dishes.

nutrient_source.title: Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, lungs, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate), sodium, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol are sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06090017), an approved primary regional source with confidence 0.95. Fatty acid fractions (saturated_fat_g 0.9, monounsaturated_fat_g 0.62, polyunsaturated_fat_g 0.48, trans_fat_g 0.0) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174 (Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, lungs, raw), an approved secondary global source. The USDA entry is the primary citation listed here as it is the approved source for the fatty acid data; HPB Foodo (cr_id F06090017) is the primary source for all other reported macros and micronutrients. Both are approved sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174 and HPB Foodo cr_id F06090017
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database — not applicable for zero-carbohydrate foods; reference sources: USDA Agricultural Research Service and Health Promotion Board Singapore
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pig lung, raw contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as confirmed by both HPB Foodo (cr_id F06090017) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database does not index zero-carbohydrate animal organ meats; gi_category is set to not_applicable and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 by definition. No GI estimation is required or appropriate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 153 mg/100 g with high potassium 303 mg/100 g; favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio in plain raw state
hypertension.guidance: Pig lung, raw has a sodium content of 153 mg/100 g, well below the 200 mg/100 g threshold, and a high potassium level of 303 mg/100 g that actively supports blood pressure regulation; however, patients must avoid high-sodium preparation sauces such as soy sauce or oyster sauce, which are commonly used in braising and can dramatically elevate the final sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; lean protein source with very low fat supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pig lung, raw contains 0.0 g carbohydrate and 0.0 g sugar per 100 g, making it glycaemically neutral and suitable for Type 2 Diabetes meal plans in plain preparation; patients should avoid sugar-based or sweet soy sauce marinades and monitor lipid profiles regularly given the high cholesterol content of 320 mg/100 g, which elevates cardiovascular risk in T2D.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Extremely high dietary cholesterol at 320 mg/100 g; arachidonic acid content from pork organ meat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pig lung, raw contains 320 mg cholesterol per 100 g, which is exceptionally high and approaches or exceeds the daily cholesterol limit recommended for cardiovascular disease patients in a single serving; individuals with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should limit or avoid consumption and consult a dietitian regarding frequency and portion size.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Arachidonic acid (omega-6) from pork organ meat amplifies inflammatory pathways; no positive anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pig lung is a pork-derived organ meat that contains arachidonic acid, an omega-6 fatty acid that promotes pro-inflammatory eicosanoid production; combined with extremely high cholesterol (320 mg/100 g) driving systemic oxidative stress and the absence of any positive anti-inflammatory factors (omega-3, antioxidants, polyphenols, or whole-food fibre above 3 g), it is not recommended for individuals managing rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pig lung, raw (cr_id F06090017) is an uncooked porcine organ meat derived from the respiratory lungs of the pig. It displays a pale pink colour with a characteristically spongy, porous texture due to the alveolar structure of lung tissue. In raw form it is rarely consumed directly; it is typically blanched, sliced, and then incorporated into soups, slow-braised dishes, or stir-fries in Chinese, Southeast Asian, and other culinary traditions. Nutritionally, pig lung is a very lean organ meat providing 85 kcal, 14.1 g protein, and only 2.7 g fat per 100 g, making it one of the lowest-fat pork organ meats available. Carbohydrate content is 0.0 g, rendering it glycaemically neutral. It is notably high in iron at 18.9 mg/100 g, which is clinically significant for iron-deficiency anaemia management, and provides 303 mg potassium per 100 g. The primary clinical concern is its extremely high cholesterol content of 320 mg/100 g, which limits its suitability for individuals with cardiovascular disease, dyslipidaemia, or elevated cardiovascular risk associated with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium at 153 mg/100 g is moderate in the raw state but rises sharply with typical braising preparations. Pig lung is also a source of arachidonic acid (omega-6), which may exacerbate inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis. It is gluten-free and dairy-free but is not suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, or kosher diets without specific certification.
common_user_queries: Is pork lung high in iron?
Is pig lung good for blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core macros, sodium, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06090017), an approved primary regional source with confidence 0.95. Fatty acid fractions (saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174 (Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, lungs, raw), an approved secondary global source, and documented in notes. Both sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero: pig lung is a pure animal organ meat containing no carbohydrate by composition; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo (cr_id F06090017) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal organ meats contain no dietary fibre; confirmed zero per both approved sources. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/absent in pork organ meats per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174). Saturated_fat_g (0.9 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.62 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.48 g) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 17174 (Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, lungs, raw) as HPB Foodo does not report fatty acid fractions separately; total fat cross-check: 0.9 + 0.62 + 0.48 = 2.0 g vs HPB-reported 2.72 g total fat — minor rounding difference within acceptable range for organ meat fatty acid profiling. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this food item; cross-mapping detailed vitamin and mineral data from USDA FoodData Central NDB 17174 to the HPB record introduces uncertainty beyond the approved adaptation scope and these fields are therefore marked as data not available. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0 by definition. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03311349 (pig lung food product) and FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Pig lung) used as defensible ontology anchors; no SNOMED CT code identified for raw pig lung. Halal status set to false — pig-derived products are not halal.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not 0) as food contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g, compliant with the low/zero-carbohydrate GI rule. gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source title and source_match_notes explicitly document the not-applicable rationale with reference to both approved nutrient sources confirming zero carbohydrate. gi_source organization references the University of Sydney GI Database as the authoritative GI body while correctly noting it does not index zero-carbohydrate foods.
